UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores information about a registered user of the Code Ascent application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the user registered.

lastActivityDate.description: The date and time of the user's last recorded activity in the app.

currentLearningPathId.description: Reference to the LearningPath the user is currently engaged in. (Relationship: LearningPath 1:N UserProfile)

LearningPath.title: LearningPath
LearningPath.description: Represents a structured curriculum or course covering a specific programming area.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningPath entity.

title.description: The name of the learning path (e.g., "Python Fundamentals").

description.description: A brief summary of what the learning path covers.

language.description: The primary programming language taught in this path (e.g., "Python", "JavaScript").

level.description: The target difficulty level of the path (e.g., "Beginner", "Intermediate", "Expert").

moduleIds.description: References to Modules included in this learning path, in their intended order. (Relationship: LearningPath 1:N Module (via array of IDs))

Module.title: Module
Module.description: A distinct unit of learning within a LearningPath, comprising lessons and exercises.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

title.description: The title of the module (e.g., "Variables and Data Types").

description.description: A summary of the concepts covered in this module.

learningPathId.description: Reference to the LearningPath this module belongs to. (Relationship: LearningPath 1:N Module)

orderInPath.description: The sequential order of this module within its parent learning path.

lessonIds.description: References to Lessons within this module, in their intended order. (Relationship: Module 1:N Lesson (via array of IDs))

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: An individual learning unit that can be an explanation, an exercise, or a quiz.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

title.description: The title of the lesson (e.g., "Introduction to Integers").

description.description: A short description of the lesson's content or objective.

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: Module 1:N Lesson)

type.description: The type of content for this lesson (e.g., "explanation", "exercise", "quiz").

contentMarkdown.description: The educational content of the lesson, formatted in Markdown (for "explanation" type lessons).

exerciseId.description: Reference to the Exercise associated with this lesson, if applicable. (Relationship: Exercise 1:1 Lesson)

Exercise.title: Exercise
Exercise.description: A coding problem or challenge designed for users to practice programming concepts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exercise entity.

title.description: The title of the coding exercise.

description.description: The full problem statement and instructions for the exercise.

language.description: The programming language required for solving this exercise.

initialCode.description: The starting code template provided to the user.

testCasesJson.description: A JSON string containing test cases (inputs and expected outputs) for automated grading.

difficulty.description: The estimated difficulty level of the exercise (e.g., "Easy", "Medium", "Hard").

skillIds.description: References to Skill entities that this exercise helps to develop or assess. (Relationship: Skill N:N Exercise)

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific programming concept, technique, or area of mastery. Used to build the visual skill tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., "Variables", "For Loops", "Data Structures").

description.description: A detailed description of what proficiency in this skill entails.

parentSkillId.description: Reference to a parent Skill, establishing a hierarchical relationship in the skill tree. (Relationship: Skill 1:N Skill (self-referencing))

prerequisiteSkillIds.description: References to other Skill entities that must be mastered before this skill. (Relationship: Skill N:N Skill (self-referencing))

UserLessonProgress.title: UserLessonProgress
UserLessonProgress.description: Tracks a user's completion status and performance for individual lessons.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserLessonProgress)

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson entity. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserLessonProgress)

status.description: The current status of the user's engagement with the lesson (e.g., "Not Started", "In Progress", "Completed").

completedAt.description: The date and time when the user completed the lesson.

score.description: The score achieved by the user for this lesson, if applicable (e.g., for quizzes or exercises).

isMastered.description: A boolean indicating if the user has demonstrated mastery of the concepts in this lesson.

UserExerciseAttempt.title: UserExerciseAttempt
UserExerciseAttempt.description: Records each attempt a user makes on an exercise, including their submitted code and outcome. This data is critical for client-side AI analysis to identify weaknesses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExerciseAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

exerciseId.description: Reference to the Exercise entity. (Relationship: Exercise 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

attemptNumber.description: A sequential number indicating which attempt this is for the given exercise.

submittedCode.description: The exact code submitted by the user for this attempt.

isCorrect.description: Boolean indicating whether the submitted code passed all test cases.

feedbackSummary.description: A high-level summary of the AI-generated feedback for this attempt, identifying key issues.

submittedAt.description: The date and time when the attempt was submitted.

timeSpentSeconds.description: The duration, in seconds, the user spent on this particular attempt.

UserSkillMastery.title: UserSkillMastery
UserSkillMastery.description: Tracks the user's current mastery level for each programming skill, feeding into the Visual Skill Mastery Tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSkillMastery entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserSkillMastery)

skillId.description: Reference to the Skill entity. (Relationship: Skill 1:N UserSkillMastery)

masteryLevel.description: A numerical value (e.g., 0-100) representing the user's proficiency in the skill.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the mastery level for this skill was last re-evaluated or updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Only the user themselves can read and update their profile. The document ID MUST match `request.auth.uid`. No additional denormalized authorization fields needed here as it is the root of user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Tracks a user's progress on individual lessons. Only the owning user can read and write this data. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path parameter and the document's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userLessonProgressId
params.description: The unique ID for the user's progress on a specific lesson.

definition.description: Records each attempt a user makes on an exercise. Only the owning user can read and write this data. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path parameter and the document's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userExerciseAttemptId
params.description: The unique ID for a specific user exercise attempt.

definition.description: Tracks a user's mastery level for each programming skill. Only the owning user can read and write this data. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path parameter and the document's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userSkillMasteryId
params.description: The unique ID for the user's mastery record of a specific skill.

definition.description: Global collection for all learning paths. This content is public and read-only for authenticated users. Writable only by administrators.

params.name: learningPathId
params.description: The unique ID of the learning path.

definition.description: Global collection for all modules. This content is public and read-only for authenticated users. Writable only by administrators. Includes 'learningPathId' for direct querying and context without parent document lookup.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique ID of the module.

definition.description: Global collection for all lessons. This content is public and read-only for authenticated users. Writable only by administrators. Includes 'moduleId' for direct querying and context.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique ID of the lesson.

definition.description: Global collection for all coding exercises. This content is public and read-only for authenticated users. Writable only by administrators.

params.name: exerciseId
params.description: The unique ID of the exercise.

definition.description: Global collection for all programming skills, forming the skill tree. This content is public and read-only for authenticated users. Writable only by administrators. Includes 'parentSkillId' and 'prerequisiteSkillIds' for direct tree navigation.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique ID of the skill.

definition.description: Collection for managing administrative roles. Each document ID corresponds to a user's UID. The existence of a document signifies admin privileges. This collection enables DBAC for secure management of global content. Read-only by non-admins, writable by existing admins.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is an administrator, matching `request.auth.uid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Structural Segregation (Mandate B)**, dividing data into two main categories: globally accessible educational content and private, user-specific progress data.

**Authorization Independence (Mandate A)** is achieved by ensuring that authorization context is denormalized where necessary. For all user-specific subcollections (e.g., `lessonProgress`, `exerciseAttempts`, `skillMastery`), the `userId` is explicitly stored within each document. This redundancy, combined with the hierarchical path (`/users/{userId}/...`), allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the document data) or `request.auth.uid == pathParam.userId` (from the URL wildcard) without resorting to expensive and complex `get()` operations. This makes rules atomic, efficient, and simpler to reason about, as access decisions for a document are self-contained.

**Querying for All Public Data (QAPs)** is supported by placing core educational content (`LearningPath`, `Module`, `Lesson`, `Exercise`, `Skill`) in top-level collections. These collections have a homogeneous security posture: read-only for all authenticated users and writable only by administrators (defined via a `roles_admin` collection). This allows clients to `list` these collections efficiently and securely, as the rules can simply permit read access for `request.auth.uid != null`. No filtering by user is needed at this level, fulfilling the 'Rules are not Filters' principle.

**Querying for User-Specific Data (QAPs)** is supported by using **Path-Based Ownership (Mandate C.1 & C.2)** for all private user data. Collections like `/users/{userId}/lessonProgress` are designed such that a user can only query their own data. A `list` operation on `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/lessonProgress` implicitly ensures that only the authenticated user's data is retrieved and that each document in the result set passes the `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` check, further ensuring robust security without complex rule logic.

**DBAC (Mandate C.4)** is implemented using an `existence over content` model for admin roles, where `/roles_admin/{adminId}` documents signify administrative privileges, allowing rules to check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`.

This structure prioritizes clarity of intent, simplifies rule writing, and ensures that the application can scale securely while providing a highly debuggable authorization model.
